info.title: SDEX Suite Windows API
info.description: This is the SDEX Suite Windows API.

post.description: Login by password.Only authenticated users are allowed to see and change the configuration of the DI server. The fastCGIServer validates with each request the user and it's rights. There are two authentication mechanisms, either of them can be used.

password.description: The name and password must be the credentials of a user with appropriate access rights.

clientNonce.description: base64-string - The client nonce is a random byte sequence (16 to 512 bytes, or 128 to 4096 bits).

expiryTime.description: The expiry time is the time at which the authentication expires if not refreshed, expressed as the number of seconds since 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Refreshing a token is possible only if not expired yet,passing a valid user and signature.

parameters.description: The user name and signature can be passed as query parameters.

oldServerNonce.description: The old server nonce is requested as an additional authentication check.

newServerNonce.description: base64-string - The new client and server nonces are to be used starting at the next request.

expiryTime.description: The expiry time is the time at which the authentication expires if not refreshed, expressed as the number of seconds since 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z.

get.summary: Get subscriptions and monitored items merging configuration
get.description: Gets the core Subscriptions and Monitored Items options that define whether monitored items are merged under compatible subscription settings.

merging.description:
Defines whether subscriptions and monitored items are merged.

false:
  Subscriptions and monitored items are never merged,
  and the relation between them is preserved.

true:
  Monitored items are merged when the following properties match:
  - Node ID
  - Attribute
  - Sampling interval
  - Queue size
  - Discard oldest flag
  - Publishing interval of the subscription

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.summary: Set subscriptions and monitored items options
post.description:

Note: If called while the system is in running mode, changing the merging parameter will trigger a restart (transition to halted mode and back to running mode).

merging.description:
Defines whether subscriptions and monitored items are merged.
false:
  Subscriptions and monitored items are never merged,
  and their relationship is preserved.

true:
  Monitored items are merged when compatible based on:
  - Node ID
  - Attribute
  - Sampling interval
  - Queue size
  - Discard oldest flag
  - Subscription publishing interval

post.description:
Updates service-based filter mask for a node.
Bitmask: - Browse: 0x01 - Read: 0x02 - Write: 0x04 - Method call: 0x08 - Subscribe: 0x10 - UI reserved: 0x1000

checked.description:
Bitmask defining allowed services for a node.

Values:
- 1    = Browse
- 2    = Read
- 4    = Write
- 8    = Method call
- 16   = Subscribe
- 4096 = UI reserved

When the corresponding bit is set, the service is allowed on that node.
The UI reserved flag has no functional meaning and is only used for UI logic.

post.description: Creates a temporary PKI for manipulating certificates. If an endpointName is given in the request, the temporary PKI is created for editing the PKI of the corresponding endpoint. The temporary PKI is created as a copy of the current PKI of that endpoint. Any other edit operation for that endpoint is aborted and the corresponding GUID is invalidated. If endpointName is null, an empty PKI is created, for a new endpoint that is expected to be created. The response contains a unique ID to be used in subsequent requests. All changes done on that temporary PKI are persisted ONLY when calling PUT or POST on runtime/opcua-server/endpoints/<endpoint> and give the pkiSessionId in the json.

put.description: Import the server own application certificate (.pfx - mime-type application/x-pkcs12). The certificate is provided as one .pfx file, which embeds a certificate and its corresponding private key. The private key in the uploaded pfx file is expected to be password-protected with the current password. If you need to upload a pfx file protected with a different password, then you should first change the password (POST on /runtime/opcua-server/edit-certificates/<session-GUID>/own/password) and then upload the pfx. The initial password for a newly-created PKI is ‘pass`. On success, the old certificate (.der and .pem) are deleted and the password is persisted.

country.description: Country must be either empty or a 2-characters ISO country code

state-or-province.description: State-or-provence and locality must be UTF-8 strings of at most 128 characters (code points are numbered)

organization.description: Organization, organization-unit, and common-name are UTF-8 strings of at most 64 characters

email.description: Email must be a valid e-mail of at most 128 characters (no non-ASCII characters are allowed)

valid-days.description: Valid-days must be between 0 and 24855 (2^31-1 seconds, or approximately 86 years)

Returns a summary list of all configured OPC UA server endpoints.

Access protection status is represented as follows:

- `certificateStatus = true` → There are certificates rejected during authentication due to access protection settings.
- `ipStatus = true` → There are client IP addresses rejected during authentication due to access protection settings.
- `endpointStatus = true` → The endpoint is temporarily or permanently disabled due to access protection settings.

put.description:
Creates a new OPC UA endpoint definition.

PKI behavior:
- If `pkiSessionId` is provided, the temporary PKI with that GUID is used as the initial PKI of the endpoint.
- If `pkiSessionId` is missing, or the temporary PKI does not contain an own certificate, a new certificate is automatically generated.
- The `pkiSessionId` is invalidated after this call.

Validation rules:
- Endpoint name must be unique.
- Endpoint URL must be in valid format.
- `description`, `max-connections`, and `access` are optional.
- `access` values must be selected from:
  - Browse
  - Read
  - Write
  - Subscribe
  - Methods

Security validation:
- If `mode-none = true` → `policy-none` must be true.
- If `mode-none = false` → `policy-none` must be false.
- If `mode-sign` or `mode-signAndEncrypt = true`, at least one secure policy must be enabled.
- If both `mode-sign` and `mode-signAndEncrypt = false`, all secure policies must be false.

Authentication validation:
- At least one of `none`, `credentials`, or `certificate` must be true.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description:
Partially updates an existing endpoint. Only changed attributes must be included.

PKI behavior:
- If `pkiSessionId` is provided, it must reference a temporary PKI associated with the current endpoint.
- The temporary PKI replaces the current endpoint PKI.
- Whether specified or not, any temporary PKI associated with the endpoint is discarded and invalidated.

Validation:
- Any included attribute follows the same validation rules as endpoint creation.

Restart behavior:
The endpoint is restarted (all client sessions closed) if any of the following change:
- `endpoint-url`
- `enabled`
- Any security parameter
- Any authentication parameter
- Own application certificate (PKI update)

Runtime behavior:
- Changing access rights has immediate effect on existing sessions.
- Existing subscriptions may stop/resume accordingly.
- Changing `max-connections` affects only future sessions.
- Changing trusted certificates affects only future sessions.

Reverse-connect:
- Can enable/disable feature.
- Defines reverse connection clients.
- If enabled, at least one enabled client must exist.
- Any reverse-connect change triggers endpoint restart.

certificatesStatus.description: Expired - at least one of the user’s certificate is expired; AboutToExpire - at least one user certificate will expire in less than 90 days; Good - all user’s certificate do have this state.

validityStatus.description: The possible values for "validityStatus" are "Good", "AboutToExpire" (will expire in less than 90 days from now) , "Expired" (already expired)

is-default-outgoing.description: At most one certificate shall have „is-default-outgoing“ on true. That certificate shall also have „private-key-available“ = true.

subject.description: Each "name" part in "subjectDN" and "issuerDN" may be "C" =country, "S" = state/county, "L" = locality (city), "O" =organization, "OU" = organization unit (department), etc. Some information is duplicate, because of historical reasons (e.g, "subjectName" is the same as "CN" under "subjectDN")

subjectAlternativeName.description: Each "name" part in "subjectAlternateName" may be "URL", "DNS", etc

password.description: Password must be either empty (no password) or otherwise between 4 and 1023 characters in length

country.description: Country must be either empty or a 2-characters ISO country code

state-or-province.description: State-or-provence and locality must be UTF-8 strings of at most 128 characters (code points are numbered)

organization.description: Organization, organization-unit, and common-name are UTF-8 strings of at most 64 characters

email.description: Email must be a valid e-mail of at most 128 characters (no non-ASCII characters are allowed)

valid-days.description: Valid-days must be between 0 and 24855 (2^31-1 seconds, or approximately 86 years)

enabled.description: The default value for 'enabled' member is false (LDAP server not used)

pkiSessionId.description: Member pkiSessionId is a GUID specifing a PKI Store previosly created to be used for secure connection.It is generally optional, but mandatory if 'secure' was set to true.

SessionName.description: Name of the HTTP UA server session type.

NodeIds.description: List of node IDs to browse.

AdditionalAttributes.description:
Optional list of additional node attributes to retrieve. If not specified, the default information for a resulting node is retrieved: `NodeId`, `DisplayName`, `NodeClass`, and `BrowseName`.
Possible values are: `Description`, `WriteMask`, `UserWriteMask`, `IsAbstract`, `Symmetric`, `InverseName`, `ContainsNoLoops`, `EventNotifier`, `Value`, `DataTypeId`, `BaseDataType`, `ValueRank`, `ArrayDimensions`, `AccessLevel`, `UserAccessLevel`, `MinSamplingInterval`, `Historizing`, `Executable`, `UserExecutable`, `DataTypeDefinition`, `RolePermissions`, `UserRolePermissions`, `AccessRestrictions`, `AccessLevelEx`.

httpuaserverBrowseNode.description: Browse result node. If `AdditionalAttributes` were requested, the response object will contain additional members where: - the key is the requested attribute name - the value is a string representation of the attribute value The returned string value must be decoded according to the corresponding OPC UA attribute type.

httpuaserverBrowseResponse.description:
Nested browse response.
Each top-level array entry corresponds to one requested NodeId. Each nested array contains the browse results for that NodeId.

SessionName.description: Name of the HTTP UA server session type.

NodeIds.description: List of node IDs to read.

AttributesToRead.description: List of attributes to read from each node. Supported attributes are: NodeId, NodeClass, BrowseName, DisplayName, Description, WriteMask, UserWriteMask, IsAbstract, Symmetric, InverseName, ContainsNoLoops, EventNotifier, Value, DataTypeId, BaseDataType, ValueRank, ArrayDimensions, AccessLevel, UserAccessLevel, MinSamplingInterval, Historizing, Executable, UserExecutable, DataTypeDefinition, RolePermissions, UserRolePermissions, AccessRestrictions, AccessLevelEx.

StatusCode.description: Status code returned for the attribute read operation.

Value.description: String representation of the attribute value.

httpuaserverReadNode.description:
Read result object for a node. Each requested attribute appears dynamically as a property name. Each attribute contains: - StatusCode - Value
If the node does not exist or is not visible in the session, only the nodeId member is returned.

httpuaserverReadResponse.description: List of read results for each requested node.

mode-none.description: if mode-none = true -> policy-none has to be true; if mode-none = false -> policy-none has to be false

mode-sign.description: if mode-sign or/and mode-signAndEncrypt = true -> policy-basic128rsa15 or/and policy-basic256 or/and policy-basic256sha256 or/and policy-Aes128Sha256RsaOaep or/and policy-Aes256Sha256RsaPss has to be true

mode-signAndEncrypt.description: if mode-sign and mode-signAndEncrypt = false -> policy-basic128rsa15 and policy-basic256 or/and policy-basic256sha256 or/and policy-Aes128Sha256RsaOaep or/and policy-Aes256Sha256RsaPss has to be false

authentication.description: Authentication validity checks by at least one of none, credentials and certificate has to be true.

pkiSessionId.description:
The temporary PKI with the given pkiSessionId is used as the initial PKI of the endpoint.If "pkiSessionId" is missing or if the temporary PKI does not contain an own certificate, a new certificate is automatically generated for the endpoint.The pkiSessionId is invalidated as a result of this call.Validity checks
- Unique name
- Correct endpoint format
- „description“, „max-connections“, „access“ are optional
- „access“ is a string array with values from the following set „Browse“, „Read“, „Write“, „Subscribe“, „Methods“

disableIncreaseFold.description: Multiplier applied to disable time after repeated failures

dropConnections.description: Drop existing connections after authentication failures exceed limits

ValueAttribute.description:
Can be one of the following:
- null value is represented as JSON null
- booleans, integers, floating point numbers, and strings are represented as their corresponding JSON equivalents
- DateTime is represented as ISO8601 string, e.g. *"2024-06-21T11:49:23.456Z"*
- ByteString is represented as a list of integers
- NodeId and QualifiedName are represented as strings, e.g. *"ns=7;s=SomeNode"* or *"42:TheAnswer"*
- LocalizedText is represented as a JSON list of JSON objects with *locale* and *text* fields
- for types derived from simple built-in types, the value is represented as the built-in type; for example, the value shall never be Duration (i=290) but rather Double (i=11)
- for types derived from Structure:
  - the value will be a JSON Object with field names as defined in the structure and with the values being the values of the fields
  - for missing UA fields, the corresponding JSON fields will be null or missing
  - if a field value can use sub-types, then the value shall be a JSON object of the form *{"dataType" : "string-node-id", "value" : json-value}*; otherwise, the value of the field is directly the representation
- for types derived from Enumeration, the value is directly the integer representation of that value
- for one or two-dimensional arrays, it shall be a JSON list of JSON values as above, or a JSON list of JSON lists of objects as above

incompatibilities.description:
Returns any incompatibilities that there are between the node and the node it is mapped to.
Alowed compatibilities:
- One is an enumeration and the other is Int32
- Both are enumerations and one is a sub type of the other
- The node is read-only and the other one is a sub-type of it
- One is a scalar of ByteString and the other one is an array of Byte

nodeClass.description: True if the nodes have different node classes.

typeDefinition.description: Is the node ID of the type definition target and is present only for nodes of class Object or Variable.

value.description: The field "payloadDefinition/destinations/displayPath/value" is not present if "payloadDefinition/destinations/displayPath/type" is "placeholder".

addressSpace.description: The "addressSpace" field is only stored by the Mqtt module and it is not validated or used in any way.The caller is responsible to manage the session type to address space mapping(see runtime/uacore/sights/conn-filtering/sessions-mapping).The session type for a publish request is the name of the publish request, prefixed by "MqttSubscriber\_".

nodeId.description: The fields "nodeId" and "displayPath" are semantically mutually exclusive.Use "nodeId" for the case a destination is fully specified by its nodeId.Use "displayPath" for the case a destination is specified by its displayPath (with placeholders).

placeholder.description: The "placeholder" member is a string in which the caller can serialize any useful information linked to the current destination. It will be just stored by the Mqtt module and it is not validated, nor used, in any way.

requestId.description: The "requestId" member is a generated unique id (GUID). It is used as a key to reference the Subscribe Request in a Publish Request triggered by MQTT definition ("dataRowTrigger"="mqttTriggered")

nodeId.description: The fields "nodeId" and "displayPath" are semantically mutually exclusive.Use "nodeId" for the case a destination is fully specified by its nodeId.Use "displayPath" for the case a destination is specified by its displayPath (with placeholders).

placeholder.description: The "placeholder" member is a string in which the caller can serialize any useful information linked to the current destination. It will be just stored by the Mqtt module and it is not validated, nor used, in any way.

addressSpace.description: The "addressSpace" field is only stored by the Mqtt module and it is not validated or used in any way. The caller is responsible to manage the session type to address space mapping (see runtime/uacore/sights/conn-filtering/sessions-mapping). The session type for a publish request is the name of the publish request, prefixed by "MqttPublisher_".

dataRowTrigger.description: samplingTimer (cannot be used with publishingMode = alarmMode); dataChange (this is the only acceptable one for publishingMode = alarmMode); conditionalSamplingTimer (cannot be used with publishingMode = alarmMode); conditionalTrigger (can be used only with  publishingMode = singleSample); advancedTrigger (can be used only with  publishingMode = singleSample); mqttTriggered (can be used only with  publishingMode = singleSample). For the case publisher is configured with dataRowTrigger=mqttTriggered the content of the previously described placeholders may have some particularities. Elements with the type=data could be defined with the display path instead of the node id. In this case, those entries wil be changed as follows "ancestors" member is not present and "nodeId" element is replaced by a new 'displayPath' member of type array.

dataRowTimeout.description: The field "dataRowTimeout" only makes sense for "dataRowTrigger"=["conditionalTrigger"|"mqttTriggered"|"advancedTrigger"] and specifies the max time to wait for the read of the data in the payload to complete.

mqttTriggerSubscriberId.description: The field "mqttTriggerSubscriberId" only makes sense for "dataRowTrigger"="mqttTriggered" and specifies the id of the configured Mqtt Subscribe request to be used as a trigger for the current publisher

samplingInterval.description: The field "samplingInterval" makes no sense for "publishingMode" other than "multipleSamples".

template.description: The field "template" contains the template for the publish message, with placeholders in the format "@name@".

placeholders.description: The filed "placeholders" contains an array of objects defining each a variable node in the address space.

displayName.description: the 'key' of this placeholder; it is used as display name in the GUI; LUA script uses this key as an argument for the exposed LUA API for retrieving the value for the current variable node.

script.description: The filed "script" contains the code to be executed by LUA as a string

timeZone.description: for "type"="timestamp", a string with possible values "utc" and "local";

nodeId.description: a string containing the node ID of the variable node(e.g., "nodeId":"ns=4;s=SomeNode"). For "type"="data", a string containing the node ID of the node whose value is to be replaced (e.g., "nodeId":"ns=4;s=SomeNode").

ancestors.description: for "type"="data", a list of strings representing the node IDs of the ancestors of the subscribed node. This is for GUI usage only; the backend will simply store and retrieve it back.

attributeId.description: a string containing the (pseudo-UA) attribute to be substituted here. For "type"="data", a string containing the (pseudo-UA) attribute to be substituted here. TBD fields describing the substitution rules for text and XML formats (how to deal with a missing value, arrays, etc)

format.description: a string describing how the value is to be formatted in the publish message.

nodeId.description: The field "nodeId" of the "triggerSource" object  is a string containing the node ID of the variable to use as trigger (e.g., "nodeId":"ns=4;s=SomeNode").

samplingInterval.description: The field "samplingInterval" defines the sampling for the variable nodes involved in the logics of the advanced trigger (the placeholders below)

addressSpace.description: The field "addresSpace" defines the address space used to localize the variables nodes used in the trigger definition

triggerValue.description: The field "triggerValue" shall be used only for the trigger definition corresponding to "dataRowTrigger"="conditionalSamplingTimer".

triggerEvent.description: The field "triggerEvent" shall be used only for the trigger definition corresponding to "dataRowTrigger"="conditionalTrigger".

dataTypeNodeId.description: The field "dataTypeNodeId" of the "triggerSource" object  is a string containing the node ID of the dataType of the variable to use as trigger (e.g. "ns=0;i=1", or simply "i=1").

selectorPath.description: The field "selectorPath" only exists if the trigger logic is fired by a sub-element of the variable (an index in case of array variable or a struct member in case of structured data type).

AuthHeader.name: Auth
AuthHeader.description:
Custom authentication header.

Format:
User=<url-encoded-user>;Signature=<url-encoded-base64-signature>

Example:
Auth: User=john%20doe;Signature=UjgyDTg2yTswMCuATEZbrRrKkdSA46zaTHXt6BO8aAg%3D

Authentication flow:
1. Call POST /runtime/core/auth/by-password
2. Compute SHA256(serverNonce + clientNonce)
3. Base64 encode the hash
4. Send Auth header on subsequent requests
